paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Studies using electrodes attached to the heads of sleepers have shown that when we sleep, we do so in two ways that alternate throughout the night. The first is rapid eye movement (REM) or active sleep. During this stage our eyes move, even though our eyelids are closed. Our muscles also twitch slightly, though they are largely paralysed so we do not hurt ourselves. In contrast, we also engage in non-REM sleep, during which we barely move at all. Most dream states, and certainly those with the most vivid dreams, happen during REM sleep.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: There is some evidence that other mammals may also dream. For example, researchers compared the brain patterns of rats running through a maze when awake with their brain patterns during REM sleep. They found the patterns were very similar and concluded that the sleeping rats were dreaming about going through the maze.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: But finding evidence of dreaming in non-mammals has proved more difficult. Their brains are very different from those of humans, and it can often be difficult to record their activity while they are sleeping. Recently, however, researchers succeeded in recording brain activity in sleeping pigeons. As in mammals, the recordings revealed both REM and non-REM sleep. Intriguingly, REM sleep activity was high in brain regions involved in processing visual information, especially images related to physical activities such as flying, which suggests that this may possibly be what the pigeons were dreaming about.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: That said, dreaming and REM sleep are unlikely to be universal in the animal kingdom. For example, sponges do not have brains, so they lack the machinery for dreaming. There are also some animals with unusual sleep patterns. These include whales and dolphins, which do not shut down their entire brain when they sleep, but only half of it, keeping the rest awake. They also show no sign of REM sleep, suggesting that they may only experience non-REM dreams, which are less vivid. This is surprising because we tend to think of whales and dolphins as having complex inner lives. It is thought that they do not experience REM sleep because during REM sleep animals are more vulnerable to extremes of temperature.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Nevertheless, in many cases REM sleep does seem to have benefits. Growing evidence from birds and mammals suggests that REM sleep and dreaming are important for forming memories and learning. It is believed that when events are replayed in dreams, this helps to integrate memories into longer-term storage. As soon as animals evolved moderately complex lifestyles, they would have needed to dream in order to manage these lifestyles.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: However, we still do not understand how this outward behaviour relates to internal experience. It seems impossible to know what it is like to be a rat or a pigeon, let alone imagine their dreamscapes. We are quick to interpret the twitching limbs and quiet barks of sleeping dogs, but the truth is that we do not know if there is an internal experience of chasing rabbits that comes along with that.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Another non-human dreamer offers insight here. In 2019, while making a documentary, David Scheel of Alaska Pacific University in the USA housed an octopus named Heidi in a tank in his living room. At one point, in the middle of the night, Heidi seemed to dream: her limbs and head moved, and her skin rapidly changed colour, as though she was pursuing a crab.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Similarly, a report recently emerged of a sleeping octopus apparently having a nightmare. Costello, as the octopus was called, thrashed around, extended his mantle as if trying to make himself look bigger, and squirted ink as though he were being attacked by a predator. The nightmare study is intriguing, says Scheel, but is only based on one animal. He argues that as well as outward behaviour, brain imaging is needed to show that the octopuses are replaying sequences of activities from their waking lives in dreams.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The trouble is that we will never be able to experience any animal's dreams. That goes for other humans' dreams too. But we can try to imagine what these dreamscapes are like by meeting animals on their own terms. For example, vision is the dominant sense for many humans, and so our dreams are heavily visual too. Dogs primarily navigate the world using smell while spiders rely much more on vibrations.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: It is likely that dreaming has served multiple purposes since the first complex animals evolved. And if this is the case, it is possible that better understanding of these purposes might shed light on the true purpose of our own dreams.

groups.title: Research into sleep and dreaming
groups.desc:
Questions 1-5
Complete the table below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 1-5 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Research into sleep and dreaming
Research findingsComment
Humanshumans experience REM sleep and non-REM sleep
in REM sleep, the eyes and muscles move
$40233$similar brain patterns were observed when active and sleepingindicative of dreaming
Pigeonswhen sleeping, pigeons displayed activity in parts of the brain that deal with $40234$ inputmay have been dreaming of flying
Whales and dolphinsstill have $40235$ their brain awake when they sleep
do not experience REM sleep, as this could affect their sensitivity to $40236$		their dreams are probably not very $40237$

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写进行睡眠与梦境研究的非人类动物名称。原文第二段明确指出，研究人员比较了老鼠（rats）在清醒时穿越迷宫和在REM睡眠期间的大脑活动模式，并发现两者非常相似，从而推断它们可能在做梦。因此，正确答案是‘rats’，与题干中‘similar brain patterns were observed when active and sleeping’完全对应。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确指出实验对象是rats，对应表格第一空。
sentences: For example, researchers compared the brain patterns of rats running through a maze when awake with their brain patterns during REM sleep.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: similar brain patterns when active and sleeping -> brain patterns of rats running through a maze when awake ... during REM sleep

central_sentences.sentence: For example, researchers compared the brain patterns of rats running through a maze when awake with their brain patterns during REM sleep.

questions.analysis: 题干询问鸽子睡眠时大脑活跃区域处理的是哪种输入。原文第三段提到，鸽子在REM睡眠期间，负责处理视觉信息（visual information）的大脑区域活动强烈，尤其是与飞行等身体活动相关的图像。这表明它们可能在梦中飞行。因此，‘visual’是唯一符合原文描述的单词，准确对应‘parts of the brain that deal with visual input’。

central_sentences.analysis: 'visual'直接对应原文'visual information'，描述鸽子大脑活跃区域。
sentences: Intriguingly, REM sleep activity was high in brain regions involved in processing visual information, especially images related to physical activities such as flying, which suggests that this may possibly be what the pigeons were dreaming about.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: brain regions involved in processing visual information -> parts of the brain that deal with visual input

central_sentences.sentence: Intriguingly, REM sleep activity was high in brain regions involved in processing visual information, especially images related to physical activities such as flying, which suggests that this may possibly be what the pigeons were dreaming about.

questions.analysis: 题干涉及鲸鱼和海豚睡眠时大脑的状态。原文第四段明确说明，这类动物睡觉时不会让整个大脑休息，而是只关闭一半（only half of it），另一半保持清醒以维持基本功能如呼吸。因此，‘half’准确对应题干中‘still have ___ their brain awake when they sleep’的描述，符合原文事实。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说鲸豚类睡觉时只关闭一半大脑，故填half。
sentences: They also show no sign of REM sleep, suggesting that they may only experience non-REM dreams, which are less vivid. This is surprising because we tend to think of whales and dolphins as having complex inner lives. It is thought that they do not experience REM sleep because during REM sleep animals are more vulnerable to extremes of temperature.
These include whales and dolphins, which do not shut down their entire brain when they sleep, but only half of it, keeping the rest awake.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: only half of it [awake] -> still have half their brain awake

central_sentences.sentence: They also show no sign of REM sleep, suggesting that they may only experience non-REM dreams, which are less vivid. This is surprising because we tend to think of whales and dolphins as having complex inner lives. It is thought that they do not experience REM sleep because during REM sleep animals are more vulnerable to extremes of temperature.
These include whales and dolphins, which do not shut down their entire brain when they sleep, but only half of it, keeping the rest awake.

questions.analysis: 题干问REM睡眠缺失可能与对什么因素的敏感性有关。原文第四段指出，鲸类不经历REM睡眠，是因为在REM阶段动物更容易受到极端温度（extremes of temperature）的影响。因此，‘temperature’是正确答案，与题干中‘affect their sensitivity to ___’形成逻辑和语义上的直接对应。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文指出REM睡眠使动物更易受温度极端影响，故填temperature。
sentences: It is thought that they do not experience REM sleep because during REM sleep animals are more vulnerable to extremes of temperature.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: vulnerable to extremes of temperature -> affect their sensitivity to temperature

central_sentences.sentence: It is thought that they do not experience REM sleep because during REM sleep animals are more vulnerable to extremes of temperature.

questions.analysis: 题干要求描述鲸鱼和海豚梦境的特点。原文第四段提到，由于它们没有REM睡眠，可能只经历non-REM dreams，而这类梦境‘less vivid’（不够生动）。因此，‘vivid’填入‘not very ___’后，完整表达为‘dreams are probably not very vivid’，与原文信息一致，符合题意。

central_sentences.analysis: 因鲸豚无REM睡眠，其梦属于non-REM类型，故不vivid。
sentences: They also show no sign of REM sleep, suggesting that they may only experience non-REM dreams, which are less vivid.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: non-REM dreams, which are less vivid -> dreams are probably not very vivid

central_sentences.sentence: They also show no sign of REM sleep, suggesting that they may only experience non-REM dreams, which are less vivid.

groups.desc:
Questions 6-13
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 6-13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Dreaming about past experiences helps us to create lasting memories of them.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述‘梦到过去经历有助于形成长久记忆’。原文第五段明确指出，当事件在梦中被重放时，有助于将记忆整合到长期存储中（integrate memories into longer-term storage），这与题干意思一致。因此，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确说做梦帮助巩固长期记忆。
sentences: It is believed that when events are replayed in dreams, this helps to integrate memories into longer-term storage.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: create lasting memories -> integrate memories into longer-term storage

central_sentences.sentence: It is believed that when events are replayed in dreams, this helps to integrate memories into longer-term storage.

questions.title: It is now possible to tell what type of dream a dog is having.
questions.analysis: 题干声称‘现在可以判断狗在做什么类型的梦’。然而，原文第六段强调：‘我们不知道狗在追逐兔子时是否真有内在体验’，并指出仅凭肢体抽动无法确定其梦境内容。这说明目前尚无法确切知道狗的梦境类型，因此题干与原文矛盾，答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说我们不知道狗的内在体验。
sentences: We are quick to interpret the twitching limbs and quiet barks of sleeping dogs, but the truth is that we do not know if there is an internal experience of chasing rabbits that comes along with that.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: tell what type -> we do not know if there is an internal experience

central_sentences.sentence: We are quick to interpret the twitching limbs and quiet barks of sleeping dogs, but the truth is that we do not know if there is an internal experience of chasing rabbits that comes along with that.

questions.title: David Scheel's documentary was influential on other research into the sleeping patterns of octopuses.
questions.analysis: 题干称David Scheel的纪录片影响了其他关于章鱼睡眠的研究。但原文第七段仅提到Scheel在拍摄纪录片时观察到章鱼Heidi的梦境行为，并未提及该纪录片对后续研究产生影响。文中无任何信息支持‘influential on other research’这一说法，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 原文未提及影响力，无法判断。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文有关于Scheel纪录片的内容，但未提及对其他研究的影响，选NG。
sentences: In 2019, while making a documentary, David Scheel of Alaska Pacific University in the USA housed an octopus named Heidi in a tank in his living room.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: was influential on other research -> (no mention)

central_sentences.sentence: In 2019, while making a documentary, David Scheel of Alaska Pacific University in the USA housed an octopus named Heidi in a tank in his living room.

questions.title: While it was asleep, the octopus called Costello reacted as if it was hunting.
questions.analysis: 题干说章鱼Costello睡觉时表现得像在捕猎。但原文第八段描述Costello的行为是‘猛力扭动、张开外套膜、喷墨’，这些反应更像是遭遇捕食者攻击（being attacked by a predator），而非主动狩猎。因此，题干与原文描述相反，答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说Costello像被攻击而非狩猎。
sentences: Costello, as the octopus was called, thrashed around, extended his mantle as if trying to make himself look bigger, and squirted ink as though he were being attacked by a predator.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: hunting -> being attacked

central_sentences.sentence: Costello, as the octopus was called, thrashed around, extended his mantle as if trying to make himself look bigger, and squirted ink as though he were being attacked by a predator.

questions.title: Scheel believes more research into octopuses' dreams should be carried out.
questions.analysis: 题干称Scheel认为应开展更多关于章鱼梦境的研究。原文第八段中，Scheel指出单一个体的观察不足为据，并强调‘除了外在行为，还需要脑成像来证明章鱼是否在梦中重演清醒时的活动序列’。这表明他支持进一步研究，因此题干与原文一致，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: Scheel认为需要更多研究如脑成像。
sentences: He argues that as well as outward behaviour, brain imaging is needed to show that the octopuses are replaying sequences of activities from their waking lives in dreams.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: more research -> brain imaging is needed

central_sentences.sentence: He argues that as well as outward behaviour, brain imaging is needed to show that the octopuses are replaying sequences of activities from their waking lives in dreams.

questions.title: We may soon be able to share the dreams of other human beings.
questions.analysis: 题干预测‘我们很快就能共享他人的梦境’。但原文第九段明确表示：‘我们永远无法体验任何动物的梦境，这也包括其他人类的梦境’（we will never be able to experience any animal's dreams. That goes for other humans' dreams too）。这直接否定了题干的可能性，因此答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说无法体验他人梦境。
sentences: The trouble is that we will never be able to experience any animal's dreams. That goes for other humans' dreams too.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: share dreams -> never be able to experience

central_sentences.sentence: The trouble is that we will never be able to experience any animal's dreams. That goes for other humans' dreams too.

questions.title: Hearing may be an important part of the dreams of some animals.
questions.analysis: 题干提出‘听觉可能是某些动物梦境的重要组成部分’。原文第九段讨论了不同动物依赖的主要感官（如人类依赖视觉、狗依赖嗅觉、蜘蛛依赖震动），但并未提及听觉（hearing）在任何动物梦境中的作用。因此，文中无相关信息支持或否定该说法，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 原文未涉及听觉在梦中的重要性。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文提到嗅觉和振动，未提及听觉，无法判断，选NG。
sentences: Dogs primarily navigate the world using smell while spiders rely much more on vibrations.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: hearing may be an important part -> (no mention of hearing)

central_sentences.sentence: Dogs primarily navigate the world using smell while spiders rely much more on vibrations.

questions.title: Interest in the reasons why humans dream has increased greatly in recent times.
questions.analysis: 题干称‘近年来人们对人类做梦原因的兴趣大大增加’。然而，全文虽探讨了多种动物的梦境及其功能，但从未提及‘interest in the reasons why humans dream’是否在近期增长。文章结尾仅推测理解动物做梦目的可能有助于揭示人类梦境的真正意义，但未涉及兴趣变化的时间趋势。因此，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文未提及人们对做梦原因的兴趣是否增加，选NG。
central_sentences.keywords: interest, reasons why humans dream, increased greatly
sentences: And if this is the case, it is possible that better understanding of these purposes might shed light on the true purpose of our own dreams.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: interest has increased greatly in recent times -> (no mention)

central_sentences.sentence: And if this is the case, it is possible that better understanding of these purposes might shed light on the true purpose of our own dreams.

content.title: Mapungubwe
content.notes: ['* palisade: typically a row of closely placed, high vertical wooden or iron posts used as a means of defence']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Mapungubwe, which was one of the first states in southern Africa, was formed by Bantu-speaking peoples who were farmers. The area controlled by the rulers of Mapungubwe had at its heart a large sandstone plateau, which was easily defended due to its inaccessibility. As with other kingdoms in the region of southern Africa, cattle herding and other types of farming brought plenty of food and a surplus that could be traded for needed goods. Archaeology has revealed extensive layers of bones and manure, which indicate that from the 9th century CE there were large cattle herds, the traditional source of political power in southern African communities. The archaeological record for the 10th century shows a marked increase in the number of domesticated cattle in the area as well as cotton cultivation and weaving, as indicated by abundant finds of spindle whorls.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The total population of Mapungubwe at its peak in the mid-13th century was around 5,000 people. The chief or king of Mapungubwe was likely the wealthiest individual in the society, and would have owned more cattle and precious materials than anyone else. The king and his advisers dwelt in a stone enclosure composed of stone walls and housing built on the highest level of the community's territory, a natural sandstone hill which is some 30 metres high and 100 metres in length. Occupation on the hill dates from the 11th century and the entire complex was surrounded by a wooden palisade, as indicated by postholes made in the rock. The rest of the community lived in mud and thatch housing spread out below the hill, although there is one stone structure here. This settlement, known as Babandyanalo, covers around 5 hectares and predates the hilltop structures.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The kings of Mapungubwe were buried at the top of the hill site in a demarcated area away from the dwellings, while other members of the community were buried at the surrounding valley level. A wooden staircase connected the two levels, the sockets for the steps being clearly visible in the sandstone cliff face. There were some grander residences dotted around the outskirts of Babandyanalo, and these probably belonged to male relatives of the king. There are many other smaller but still impressive sites across the Mapungubwe plateau, which are located anywhere from 15 to 100 kilometres from the major hill site. Containing stone residences and walls, they likely belonged to local chiefs who acted as servants to the king.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The Mapungubwe plateau has a very high number of carnivore animal remains and ivory splinters, suggesting that the skins of these large animals and ivory elephant tusks were accumulated, probably for trade with coastal areas reached by the Limpopo River. The presence of glass beads, almost certainly from India, indicate there was trade of some sort with other states on the coast who, in turn, traded with merchants travelling from India by sea. Mapungubwe also benefitted from locally-sourced copper and the gold trade as it passed from the kingdom of Great Zimbabwe, situated to the north of Mapungubwe, to the coastal city of Kosala. It is likely that trade links led to a strengthening of political authority in order to control and even monopolise these lucrative interregional connections.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Archaeological discoveries reveal that pottery was produced on a scale large enough to suggest the presence of professional potters, and is another indicator of the prosperity of Mapungubwe society. Archaeological finds include spherical vessels with short necks, beakers, and bowls, many of which have decorative stamps. There are also ceramic discs, and whistles. In addition, cattle, sheep, and goat figurines, and small figures of highly stylised humans with elongated bodies and short limbs have been found. The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known. Other discoveries include small jewellery items made from locally sourced copper.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Beautifully decorated artefacts made of gold have also been found at Mapungubwe. A type of decoration, found nowhere else except Great Zimbabwe, involved the crafting of gold into small rectangular sheets and carving geometrical patterns into it. These sheets were then used to cover wooden objects using small tacks, also made of gold. One such object that has been discovered may have been a sceptre, while additional evidence of local gold-working is a rhinoceros figurine made from small hammered sheets, and thousands of small gold beads. These objects were all found at the royal burial site and date to c. 1150. They are the first known indicators that gold had an intrinsic value of its own in southern Africa.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The kingdom of Mapungubwe was already in decline by the late 13th century, probably because overpopulation placed too much stress on local resources, a situation that may have been brought to a crisis point by a series of droughts. Trade routes may also have shifted northwards. Certainly, the areas that now prospered were to the north, such as Great Zimbabwe.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-19
Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A-G.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A-G, in boxes 14-19 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: a mention of the uncertainty regarding the purpose of certain objects
questions.analysis: 题干中的‘uncertainty regarding the purpose of certain objects’对应原文E段中的‘The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known’。句中‘precise function is not known’明确表达了对这些陶俑用途的不确定性，因此答案为E。

central_sentences.analysis: E段提到人形陶俑用途不明，对应‘uncertainty’
sentences: The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: uncertainty regarding the purpose -> precise function is not known

central_sentences.sentence: The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known.

questions.title: the likelihood that a climatic factor increased the problems Mapungubwe faced
questions.analysis: 题干中的‘climatic factor increased the problems’对应G段提到的‘a series of droughts’可能使资源压力达到危机点。干旱属于气候因素，且与Mapungubwe面临的问题（资源紧张）直接相关，因此答案为G。

central_sentences.reason: 段落G开头提到干旱加剧问题。
central_sentences.analysis: 该句指出干旱（气候因素）可能加剧危机。
sentences: The kingdom of Mapungubwe was already in decline by the late 13th century, probably because overpopulation placed too much stress on local resources, a situation that may have been brought to a crisis point by a series of droughts.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: climatic factor -> a series of droughts

central_sentences.sentence: The kingdom of Mapungubwe was already in decline by the late 13th century, probably because overpopulation placed too much stress on local resources, a situation that may have been brought to a crisis point by a series of droughts.

questions.title: a mention of the location where members of the king's family are thought to have lived
questions.analysis: 题干询问国王家族成员的居住地，C段指出‘There were some grander residences dotted around the outskirts of Babandyanalo, and these probably belonged to male relatives of the king’，其中‘male relatives of the king’即国王家族成员，因此答案为C。

central_sentences.analysis: C段称外围宏伟住宅可能属国王男性亲属
sentences: There were some grander residences dotted around the outskirts of Babandyanalo, and these probably belonged to male relatives of the king.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: members of the king's family -> male relatives of the king

central_sentences.sentence: There were some grander residences dotted around the outskirts of Babandyanalo, and these probably belonged to male relatives of the king.

questions.title: a reference to people who brought goods by ship
questions.analysis: 题干中的‘people who brought goods by ship’对应D段中的‘merchants travelling from India by sea’，他们通过海路将玻璃珠等商品带到沿海地区，再经由贸易传入Mapungubwe，因此答案为D。

central_sentences.analysis: 该句提及从印度乘船来的商人。
sentences: The presence of glass beads, almost certainly from India, indicate there was trade of some sort with other states on the coast who, in turn, traded with merchants travelling from India by sea.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: people who brought goods by ship -> merchants travelling from India by sea

central_sentences.sentence: The presence of glass beads, almost certainly from India, indicate there was trade of some sort with other states on the coast who, in turn, traded with merchants travelling from India by sea.

questions.title: an estimate of the size to which the Mapungubwe community grew
questions.analysis: 题干要求估计Mapungubwe社区的规模，B段明确指出‘The total population of Mapungubwe at its peak in the mid-13th century was around 5,000 people’，这是对社区规模的直接描述，因此答案为B。

central_sentences.analysis: B段给出鼎盛时期人口约5000人
sentences: The total population of Mapungubwe at its peak in the mid-13th century was around 5,000 people.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: size of community -> total population...around 5,000

central_sentences.sentence: The total population of Mapungubwe at its peak in the mid-13th century was around 5,000 people.

questions.title: a mention of agricultural produce being exchanged for other items
questions.analysis: 题干中的‘agricultural produce being exchanged for other items’对应A段提到的‘a surplus that could be traded for needed goods’，农业盈余用于交换所需物品，符合题意，因此答案为A。

central_sentences.analysis: 该句提到剩余农产品用于交换所需物品。
sentences: As with other kingdoms in the region of southern Africa, cattle herding and other types of farming brought plenty of food and a surplus that could be traded for needed goods.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: agricultural produce -> surplus from farming; exchanged for other items -> traded for needed goods

central_sentences.sentence: As with other kingdoms in the region of southern Africa, cattle herding and other types of farming brought plenty of food and a surplus that could be traded for needed goods.

groups.desc:
Questions 20 and 21
Choose TWO letters, A-E.
Write the correct letters in boxes 20 and 21 on your answer sheet.

collect_option.title: The archaeological record reveals information about gold and the kingdom of Mapungubwe. Which TWO pieces of information are mentioned by the writer?

list.text: Not everyone in Mapungubwe used gold as a form of payment.
Items of gold were placed close to where Mapungubwe kings were buried.
The most valuable item discovered in Mapungubwe was a sceptre made of gold.
The way gold was decorated in Mapungubwe was also practised in another kingdom.
Working with gold was a respected occupation in the Mapungubwe community.

questions.analysis: 题干要求选出关于黄金的两项信息。B项对应F段‘These objects were all found at the royal burial site’，说明黄金物品靠近国王墓葬；D项对应‘A type of decoration, found nowhere else except Great Zimbabwe’，表明该装饰技法也在另一王国使用。因此答案为B和D。

central_sentences.analysis: B和D均被提及：黄金器物出土于王陵，装饰技法亦见于大津巴布韦
sentences: These objects were all found at the royal burial site and date to c. 1150.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: placed near kings' burial -> found at the royal burial site; decoration practised elsewhere -> found nowhere else except Great Zimbabwe

central_sentences.sentence: These objects were all found at the royal burial site and date to c. 1150.

questions.analysis: 本题与第20题共享相同原文依据。F段指出黄金制品出土于王室墓地（支持B项），且其装饰风格仅见于Great Zimbabwe（支持D项）。这两点均被明确提及，因此答案仍为B和D。

central_sentences.analysis: 同Q20，两个正确选项依据相同原文句
sentences: These objects were all found at the royal burial site and date to c. 1150.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: placed near kings' burial -> found at the royal burial site; decoration practised elsewhere -> found nowhere else except Great Zimbabwe

central_sentences.sentence: These objects were all found at the royal burial site and date to c. 1150.

groups.title: Archaeological discoveries
groups.desc:
Questions 22-26
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 22-26 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: The Mapungubwe community's $40254$ is indicated by the amount of professionally made pottery discovered at the site. Many of these objects, such as beakers and bowls, are highly decorated and have been marked with stamps. Other finds include round ceramic objects, $40255$ and figures of various animals, as well as models of people with stretched $40256$. It is possible that these had a role in ceremonies to honour $40257$. In addition, pieces of $40258$ made from a local metal have been found at the site.

questions.analysis: 题干中的‘professionally made pottery’对应E段‘pottery was produced on a scale large enough to suggest the presence of professional potters’，并进一步说明这是‘another indicator of the prosperity’，因此空格应填‘prosperity’。

central_sentences.analysis: E段明确将专业制陶视为繁荣标志
sentences: Archaeological discoveries reveal that pottery was produced on a scale large enough to suggest the presence of professional potters, and is another indicator of the prosperity of Mapungubwe society.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: amount of pottery -> indicator of prosperity

central_sentences.sentence: Archaeological discoveries reveal that pottery was produced on a scale large enough to suggest the presence of professional potters, and is another indicator of the prosperity of Mapungubwe society.

questions.analysis: 题干中的‘round ceramic objects’对应E段提到的‘ceramic discs’，而该句后半部分紧接着提到‘and whistles’，两者为并列发现物，因此第23空应填‘whistles’。

central_sentences.analysis: E段列出陶瓷圆盘和哨子为并列发现物
sentences: There are also ceramic discs, and whistles.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: round ceramic objects -> ceramic discs

central_sentences.sentence: There are also ceramic discs, and whistles.

questions.analysis: 题干中的‘models of people with stretched bodies’对应E段描述的‘small figures of highly stylised humans with elongated bodies’，其中‘elongated bodies’与‘stretched bodies’为同义表达，因此答案为‘bodies’。

central_sentences.analysis: 该句描述人偶有拉长的身体。
sentences: small figures of highly stylised humans with elongated bodies and short limbs
central_sentences.paraphrasing: stretched bodies -> elongated bodies

central_sentences.sentence: small figures of highly stylised humans with elongated bodies and short limbs

questions.analysis: 题干中的‘ceremonies to honour’对应E段‘used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors’，‘ancestors’是祭祀对象，与‘honour’语义一致，因此第25空应填‘ancestors’。

central_sentences.analysis: 该句提到人偶可能用于祭祀祖先。
sentences: The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: role in ceremonies to honour ancestors -> used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors

central_sentences.sentence: The figures may have been used in ceremonies as offerings to ancestors, but their precise function is not known.

questions.analysis: 题干中的‘pieces of...made from a local metal’对应E段末句‘small jewellery items made from locally sourced copper’，‘jewellery’是原文中明确提到的物品类别，且铜为当地金属，因此答案为‘jewellery’。

central_sentences.analysis: E段末句提到铜制小件珠宝
sentences: Other discoveries include small jewellery items made from locally sourced copper.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: local metal -> locally sourced copper

central_sentences.sentence: Other discoveries include small jewellery items made from locally sourced copper.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In many countries in the West, hysteria about the future of artificial intelligence (AI) is everywhere. There seems to be no shortage of sensationalist news about how AI could cure diseases, accelerate human innovation and improve human creativity. Just looking at the media headlines, you might think that we are already living in a future where AI has infiltrated every aspect of society.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: While it is undeniable that AI has opened up a wealth of promising opportunities, it has also led to the emergence of a mindset that can be best described as AI solutionism. This is the philosophy that, given enough data, machine learning algorithms can solve all of humanity's problems. But, in fact, instead of supporting AI progress, this mindset actually jeopardises the value of machine intelligence by disregarding important AI safety principles and setting unrealistic expectations about what AI can really do for humanity.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In only a few years, AI solutionism has made its way from the technology evangelists' mouths in Silicon Valley in California to the minds of government officials and policymakers around the world. The pendulum has swung from the dystopian notion that AI will destroy humanity to the utopian belief that our algorithmic saviour is here.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: We are now seeing governments pledge support to national AI initiatives and compete in a technological race to dominate the burgeoning machine-learning sector. While many politicians proclaim the transformative effects of the coming AI revolution, they fail to realise the complexity around deploying advanced machine learning systems in the real world.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: One of the most promising varieties of AI technologies are neural networks. This form of machine learning is loosely modelled on the neuronal structure of the human brain, but on a much smaller scale. But what many politicians do not understand is that simply adding a neural network to a problem will not automatically mean that you will find a solution. Similarly, adding a neural network to a system of government does not mean it will be instantaneously more inclusive or fair.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: AI systems need a lot of data to function, but the public sector typically does not have the appropriate data infrastructure to support advanced machine learning. Most of the data remains stored in offline archives. The few digitised sources of data that exist tend to be buried in bureaucracy. More often than not, data is spread across different government departments that each require special permissions to be accessed. Above all, the public sector typically lacks the human talent with the right technological capabilities to fully reap the benefits of machine intelligence.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: For these reasons, the sensationalism over AI has attracted many critics. Stuart Russell, a professor of computer science at the University of California, Berkeley, has long advocated a more sensible and realistic approach that focuses on simple everyday applications of AI instead of the hypothetical takeover by super-intelligent robots. Similarly, Rodney Brooks, professor of robotics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology, writes that almost all innovations in robotics and AI take far, far, longer to be really widely deployed than people in the field and outside the field imagine.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: One of the many difficulties in deploying machine learning systems is that AI is extremely susceptible to adversarial attacks. This means that a malicious AI can target another AI to make it behave in a certain way, such as forcing it to make wrong predictions. Many researchers have warned against the rolling out of AI without appropriate security standards and defence mechanisms. Still, AI security remains an often overlooked topic when machine learning systems are installed.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: If we are to reap the benefits and minimise the potential harms of AI, we must start thinking about how machine learning can be meaningfully applied to specific areas of government, business and society. This means we need to have a discussion about AI ethics and the distrust that many people have towards machine learning.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Most importantly, we need to be aware of the limitations of AI and where people still need to take the lead. Instead of painting an unrealistic picture of the power of AI, it is important to take a step back and separate the actual technological capabilities of AI from fantasy.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: The medical profession has also recognised the drawbacks to AI. The IBM Watson for Oncology programme was a piece of AI that was meant to help doctors treat cancer. Even though it was developed to deliver the best recommendations, human experts found it hard to trust the machine. As a result, the AI programme was abandoned in most hospitals where it was trialled.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: Similar difficulties arose in the legal domain when algorithms were used in courts in the US to sentence criminals. An algorithm calculated risk assessment scores and advised judges on the sentencing. The system was found to amplify structural racial discrimination and was later abandoned.

paragraphs.label: M
paragraphs.text: There are some crucial lessons here for everyone aiming to boost investments in national AI programmes. These examples demonstrate that there is no AI solution for everything. Using AI simply for the sake of AI may not always be productive or useful, and not every issue is best addressed by applying machine intelligence to it. All solutions come with a cost and not everything that can be automated should be.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-29
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-29 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What is the writer doing in the first paragraph?

options.text: predicting the future impact of AI
describing a public perception of AI
outlining some possible benefits of AI
highlighting the breadth of the influence of AI

questions.analysis: 题干询问作者在第一段的写作目的。原文首段提到西方国家对人工智能（AI）未来的‘hysteria’（歇斯底里）以及媒体头条营造出AI已渗透社会各方面的印象，这表明作者是在描述公众通过媒体报道所形成的对AI的夸张认知，而非预测未来或列举其好处。因此，选项B‘describing a public perception of AI’准确对应了该段主旨。

central_sentences.reason: 正确选项B对应描述公众对AI的看法，而非预测或列举好处
central_sentences.analysis: 首段描述公众通过媒体形成的对AI的夸张认知
sentences: In many countries in the West, hysteria about the future of artificial intelligence (AI) is everywhere.
Just looking at the media headlines, you might think that we are already living in a future where AI has infiltrated every aspect of society.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干问作者在第一段做什么 -> 原文描述媒体如何呈现AI

central_sentences.sentence: In many countries in the West, hysteria about the future of artificial intelligence (AI) is everywhere.
Just looking at the media headlines, you might think that we are already living in a future where AI has infiltrated every aspect of society.

questions.title: When discussing AI solutionism in the second paragraph, the writer

options.text: weighs up some pros and cons.

questions.analysis: 第二段讨论‘AI solutionism’时，作者明确指出这种思维模式‘jeopardises the value of machine intelligence’并‘setting unrealistic expectations’，即不仅忽视AI安全原则，还设定了不切实际的期望。这清楚地表明作者是在指出该理念所蕴含的风险，而非介绍其起源、支持者或权衡利弊。因此，正确选项为A‘points out a risk involved.’。

central_sentences.reason: 选项A‘指出风险’与原文jeopardises和unrealistic expectations完全对应
central_sentences.analysis: 第二段明确指出AI solutionism会危害AI价值并设不切实际期望
sentences: But, in fact, instead of supporting AI progress, this mindset actually jeopardises the value of machine intelligence by disregarding important AI safety principles and setting unrealistic expectations about what AI can really do for humanity.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干问讨论AI solutionism时作者行为 -> 原文指出其风险

central_sentences.sentence: But, in fact, instead of supporting AI progress, this mindset actually jeopardises the value of machine intelligence by disregarding important AI safety principles and setting unrealistic expectations about what AI can really do for humanity.

questions.title: In the fourth paragraph, the writer suggests that many politicians may

options.text: have failed to appreciate the true potential of AI initiatives.
have misunderstood the function of the machine-learning sector.
be unaware of the challenges of implementing national AI initiatives.
be too keen to enter the race to dominate the machine-learning sector.

questions.analysis: 第四段提到许多政客宣称AI革命的变革性影响，却‘fail to realise the complexity around deploying advanced machine learning systems in the real world’，说明他们并未意识到在现实中推行国家级AI计划所面临的复杂挑战。选项C‘be unaware of the challenges of implementing national AI initiatives’正是对此句的准确转述，因此为正确答案。

central_sentences.reason: 选项C‘未意识到实施挑战’精准对应fail to realise the complexity
central_sentences.analysis: 第四段指出政客未认识到现实中部署AI系统的复杂性
sentences: While many politicians proclaim the transformative effects of the coming AI revolution, they fail to realise the complexity around deploying advanced machine learning systems in the real world.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干问政客可能如何 -> 原文说他们未意识到部署AI的复杂性

central_sentences.sentence: While many politicians proclaim the transformative effects of the coming AI revolution, they fail to realise the complexity around deploying advanced machine learning systems in the real world.

groups.title: AI in government, medicine and the law
groups.desc:
Questions 30-35
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-I, below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, in boxes 30-35 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Neural networks are a promising area of AI technology for governments. However, many politicians overestimate their capabilities, believing that the mere addition of a neural network will produce solutions and promote $40262$.

Most public sector organisations have not set up the necessary $40263$ to manage the huge amount of data required to enable AI to function. Complex bureaucracy is another issue, as each person involved needs $40264$ to access the relevant data, which is often spread across different departments. But the main problem is that few public sector employees have the $40265$ to take full advantage of machine intelligence.

The medical profession experimented with an AI programme, but their experts had little faith in its $40266$, and the programme was abandoned. US courts also abandoned the use of algorithms when it was found that these reflected and magnified the existing $40267$ within the legal profession.

questions.analysis: 题干中‘promote ___’对应第五段末句‘adding a neural network to a system of government does not mean it will be instantaneously more inclusive or fair’。此处‘inclusive or fair’强调公平与包容，而选项F‘equality’是对这一概念的合理概括，因此30题应填F。

central_sentences.reason: F（equality）是对inclusive or fair的合理概括
central_sentences.analysis: 添加神经网络不会自动使政府更包容或公平，对应equality
sentences: Similarly, adding a neural network to a system of government does not mean it will be instantaneously more inclusive or fair.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干promote ___ -> 原文more inclusive or fair

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, adding a neural network to a system of government does not mean it will be instantaneously more inclusive or fair.

questions.analysis: 第31题空格需填入公共部门缺乏的‘necessary ___ to manage data’。第六段指出‘the public sector typically does not have the appropriate data infrastructure to support advanced machine learning’，其中‘data infrastructure’指支撑AI运行的数据架构或框架，对应选项G‘framework’，故答案为G。

central_sentences.reason: G（framework）对应infrastructure，指支撑AI的数据架构
central_sentences.analysis: 公共部门缺乏管理数据所需的基础设施，即framework
sentences: the public sector typically does not have the appropriate data infrastructure to support advanced machine learning.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干necessary ___ to manage data -> 原文does not have appropriate data infrastructure

central_sentences.sentence: the public sector typically does not have the appropriate data infrastructure to support advanced machine learning.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写访问数据所需的条件。第六段提到数据‘spread across different government departments that each require special permissions to be accessed’，‘special permissions’即特殊许可或批准，对应选项I‘approval’，因此32题答案为I。

central_sentences.analysis: 访问数据需特殊许可，即approval
sentences: Most of the data remains stored in offline archives. The few digitised sources of data that exist tend to be buried in bureaucracy. More often than not, data is spread across different government departments that each require special permissions to be accessed.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干needs ___ to access data -> 原文require special permissions

central_sentences.sentence: Most of the data remains stored in offline archives. The few digitised sources of data that exist tend to be buried in bureaucracy. More often than not, data is spread across different government departments that each require special permissions to be accessed.

questions.analysis: 第33题空格指公共部门员工缺乏的能力。第六段末句指出‘the public sector typically lacks the human talent with the right technological capabilities’，‘technological capabilities’即技术技能，对应选项C‘skills’，因此答案为C。

central_sentences.analysis: 缺乏具备技术能力的人才，即skills
central_sentences.keywords: lacks the human talent, technological capabilities
sentences: Above all, the public sector typically lacks the human talent with the right technological capabilities to fully reap the benefits of machine intelligence.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干have the ___ to take advantage -> 原文lacks human talent with right technological capabilities

central_sentences.sentence: Above all, the public sector typically lacks the human talent with the right technological capabilities to fully reap the benefits of machine intelligence.

questions.analysis: 题干中‘little faith in its ___’对应第十一段‘human experts found it hard to trust the machine’，医生难以信任AI的可靠性，导致项目被弃用。‘trust’在此语境下等同于对AI‘reliability’（可靠性）的怀疑，因此34题应选A。

central_sentences.analysis: 医生难以信任AI的可靠性，故放弃使用
sentences: Even though it was developed to deliver the best recommendations, human experts found it hard to trust the machine. As a result, the AI programme was abandoned in most hospitals where it was trialled.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干little faith in its ___ -> 原文hard to trust the machine

central_sentences.sentence: Even though it was developed to deliver the best recommendations, human experts found it hard to trust the machine. As a result, the AI programme was abandoned in most hospitals where it was trialled.

questions.analysis: 第35题空格需填入算法所反映和放大的问题。第十二段明确指出‘The system was found to amplify structural racial discrimination’，即系统放大了法律体系中固有的结构性偏见，而选项D‘prejudices’正是对‘racial discrimination’这类偏见的概括，故答案为D。

central_sentences.reason: D（prejudices）对应racial discrimination这一结构性偏见
central_sentences.analysis: 算法放大了法律系统中已有的偏见
sentences: The system was found to amplify structural racial discrimination and was later abandoned.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干reflected and magnified existing ___ -> 原文amplify structural racial discrimination

central_sentences.sentence: The system was found to amplify structural racial discrimination and was later abandoned.

groups.desc:
Questions 36-39
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36-39 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Stuart Russell's proposals regarding the use of AI are impractical.
questions.analysis: 题干称Stuart Russell的提议‘impractical’，但第七段作者明确表示Russell倡导的是‘a more sensible and realistic approach’，聚焦AI的日常应用而非幻想超级智能接管。作者显然支持其务实立场，因此认为其提议不切实际的说法与原文相悖，答案为NO。

central_sentences.reason: 作者支持Russell的观点，因此陈述与其相悖，选NO
central_sentences.analysis: 作者赞同Russell务实做法，故说其impractical是错误的
central_sentences.keywords: Stuart Russell, sensible and realistic approach
sentences: Stuart Russell, a professor of computer science at the University of California, Berkeley, has long advocated a more sensible and realistic approach that focuses on simple everyday applications of AI instead of the hypothetical takeover by super-intelligent robots.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干称其proposals impractical -> 原文advocated sensible and realistic approach

central_sentences.sentence: Stuart Russell, a professor of computer science at the University of California, Berkeley, has long advocated a more sensible and realistic approach that focuses on simple everyday applications of AI instead of the hypothetical takeover by super-intelligent robots.

questions.title: Rodney Brooks' view has attracted unfair criticism from supporters of AI.
questions.analysis: 题干称Rodney Brooks的观点‘attracted unfair criticism’，但第七段仅引述其观点——AI和机器人创新实际部署所需时间远超预期，并未提及他是否受到批评，更无‘unfair criticism’的相关信息。因此无法判断作者看法，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 未提及批评，无法判断，选NOT GIVEN
central_sentences.analysis: 原文仅陈述Brooks观点，未提及其是否遭批评
sentences: Similarly, Rodney Brooks, professor of robotics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology, writes that almost all innovations in robotics and AI take far, far, longer to be really widely deployed than people in the field and outside the field imagine.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干称其view attracted unfair criticism -> 原文未提任何批评

central_sentences.sentence: Similarly, Rodney Brooks, professor of robotics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology, writes that almost all innovations in robotics and AI take far, far, longer to be really widely deployed than people in the field and outside the field imagine.

questions.title: Nowadays, the need to protect AI systems is always taken into account when they are set up.
questions.analysis: 题干声称‘the need to protect AI systems is always taken into account’，但第八段明确指出‘AI security remains an often overlooked topic when machine learning systems are installed’，即AI安全常被忽视，与‘always taken into account’直接矛盾，因此答案为NO。

central_sentences.reason: 陈述与原文直接矛盾，选NO
central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确说AI安全常被忽视，与always taken into account矛盾
sentences: Still, AI security remains an often overlooked topic when machine learning systems are installed.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干称need to protect AI is always taken into account -> 原文AI security remains overlooked

central_sentences.sentence: Still, AI security remains an often overlooked topic when machine learning systems are installed.

questions.title: In order to benefit from AI and minimise the harms, we have to explore people's concerns about its use.
questions.analysis: 题干提到‘explore people's concerns about its use’，第九段作者指出‘we need to have a discussion about AI ethics and the distrust that many people have towards machine learning’，其中‘distrust’即公众对AI的担忧，与题干中的‘concerns’一致，因此答案为YES。

central_sentences.analysis: 作者主张讨论AI伦理及公众不信任问题
sentences: This means we need to have a discussion about AI ethics and the distrust that many people have towards machine learning.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 题干explore people's concerns -> 原文discussion about AI ethics and distrust

central_sentences.sentence: This means we need to have a discussion about AI ethics and the distrust that many people have towards machine learning.

groups.desc:
Question 40
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in box 40 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What would be a suitable subtitle for Reading Passage 3?

options.text: How to make the most of what AI has to offer
Why AI may not be the answer to our problems
Why governments should not invest in AI systems
How AI could improve the efficiency of the public sector

questions.analysis: 全文通过政府、医疗、司法等多个领域的案例，反复强调AI并非万能，存在局限性，且盲目应用可能带来风险。结尾段总结‘there is no AI solution for everything’，明确反对将AI视为解决一切问题的灵丹妙药。选项B‘Why AI may not be the answer to our problems’精准概括了这一核心论点，因此为最佳副标题。

central_sentences.reason: B选项‘Why AI may not be the answer to our problems’准确概括全文批判AI万能论的主旨
central_sentences.analysis: 全文通过多领域案例说明AI有局限，B最贴切
central_sentences.keywords: no AI solution for everything, limitations
sentences: These examples demonstrate that there is no AI solution for everything.
Using AI simply for the sake of AI may not always be productive or useful, and not every issue is best addressed by applying machine intelligence to it.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: subtitle应概括主旨 -> 全文强调AI并非万能

central_sentences.sentence: These examples demonstrate that there is no AI solution for everything.
Using AI simply for the sake of AI may not always be productive or useful, and not every issue is best addressed by applying machine intelligence to it.
